message:
Replace DecodingText scramble with clean motion entrance, refine hero layout

DecodingText complete rewrite (components/decoding-text.tsx):
  Replaced the per-character scrambling animation with a simple
  framer-motion fade+slide entrance (opacity 0→1, y 10→0). The
  previous implementation — even after the ref-based rewrite —
  was still causing subtle interaction issues with nearby video
  compositing layers due to rapid textContent mutations triggering
  browser repaints. The new approach uses a single CSS transform
  animation that the browser can promote to its own compositor
  layer, completely eliminating the video flashing problem.

  The component now accepts text, className, and delay props.
  The duration prop is accepted but unused (kept for API compat
  with existing call sites). The cubic-bezier easing [0.215, 0.61,
  0.355, 1.0] provides a smooth deceleration that feels natural
  for text appearing.

Homepage hero restructure (app/page.tsx):
  - "The" and "Terminal Kernel." use plain DecodingText fade-in
    outside the FrankenGlitch wrapper (no chromatic aberration
    competing with the video)
  - "Monster" gets FrankenGlitch with red color (text-red-500)
    for visual emphasis — the glitch effect is isolated to just
    this word, creating a focal point without overwhelming the
    entire heading
  - Added text-left to h1 for consistent alignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
